title: MediaCMS
description: Modern, fully featured open-source video and media content management system for building self-hosted media portals and streaming sites.
installation: This template runs the PRIMARY MediaCMS application container ONLY (mediacms/mediacms). MediaCMS is a Django + Celery MULTI-CONTAINER stack and is NOT complete with this pod alone. By default this pod is configured as the WEB front end (ENABLE_UWSGI=yes, ENABLE_NGINX=yes serving HTTP on port 80), with migrations and Celery DISABLED so they can run as dedicated apps. REQUIRED before/at first boot: 1) Run database migrations ONCE - deploy a one-shot companion app from the SAME image mediacms/mediacms with env ENABLE_UWSGI=no, ENABLE_NGINX=no, ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=no, ENABLE_CELERY_SHORT=no, ENABLE_CELERY_LONG=no, ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=yes, ADMIN_USER=admin, ADMIN_EMAIL=admin@localhost and ADMIN_PASSWORD set to a strong value (this also creates the first superuser). Alternatively set ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=yes on THIS web pod for the very first deploy, then set it back to no. 2) Rotate the Postgres password (template default is the slug 'mediacms') and set a real ADMIN_PASSWORD. Valkey/Redis is password-less in-cluster. MANDATORY COMPANION SERVICES to deploy as SEPARATE apps from the SAME image mediacms/mediacms, sharing the SAME Postgres + Valkey + the /home/mediacms/mediacms/media_files volume (use RWX storage so uploads/transcodes are visible to all): (a) Celery WORKER for short tasks - env ENABLE_UWSGI=no, ENABLE_NGINX=no, ENABLE_CELERY_SHORT=yes, ENABLE_CELERY_LONG=yes, ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=no, ENABLE_MIGRATIONS=no (handles thumbnailing, encoding/transcoding and notifications - REQUIRED or uploaded videos never finish processing); (b) Celery BEAT scheduler - env ENABLE_CELERY_BEAT=yes with all other ENABLE_* = no (periodic cleanup/maintenance jobs). All companion pods need the identical POSTGRES_* and REDIS_LOCATION env vars as this web pod. Route public ingress to THIS web pod (it runs the bundled nginx). Set FRONTEND_HOST to your public https URL so absolute media/asset links resolve correctly. Heavy video transcoding is CPU/RAM intensive - size the worker pod accordingly. For production at scale media_files can be offloaded to S3 (e.g. the RustFS add-on) via Django storages, but the default uses the local volume.
